events.label: reasoning
events.text:
The scenario is about brazing & evacuation in HVAC-R. The observed field condition is "vacuum not held at target microns (decay test - read the gauge, FD)". This means the vacuum decay test failed - the system is not holding at the required micron level, indicating leaks or moisture/non-condensables still present.

This is a non-compliant condition. The system cannot proceed to charging until proper evacuation is achieved. Per manufacturer specs and ACCA standards, the system must hold at target microns (typically 500 microns or below) for a decay test.

Decision: fail
Risk: medium-high (improper evacuation can lead to compressor failure, system contamination)
Work stage: rough-complete
Component conditions: non-compliant (the evacuation work doesn't meet spec)
Percent complete: maybe 85% (roughed in but evacuation failed)
Value: could represent the micron reading but we don't have exact number - the scenario says "not held at target microns" so I'll leave 0 or note it's above target.

Let me construct the JSON.

  "findings": [
    "Vacuum decay test failed — system did not hold at target microns, indicating residual moisture, non-condensables, or a leak in the brazed/assembly joints",
    "Evacuation does not meet manufacturer or ACCA acceptance criteria for system dehydration prior to refrigerant charging"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Pressurize system with dry nitrogen and leak-test all brazed joints and mechanical connections to locate source of decay",
    "Repair any identified leaks or defective braze joints, then re-evacuate using a proper vacuum pump with micron gauge",
    "Re-perform vacuum decay test and confirm system holds at or below manufacturer-specified target microns (commonly 500 microns) before charging",
    "Do not introduce refrigerant charge until evacuation acceptance criteria are met"
  ],
  "rationale": "The vacuum decay test did not hold at target microns, which means the system has not been properly evacuated and may contain leaks, moisture, or non-condensables. Charging a system in this state risks compressor damage and violates manufacturer requirements and ACCA evacuation standards. Work must be corrected before proceeding.",
  "references": [
    "Manufacturer specifications for evacuation and vacuum decay acceptance",
    "ACCA Standard 5 (HVAC Quality Installation) — evacuation and dehydration requirements",
    "EPA Section 608 — proper refrigerant handling practices; system must be leak-free and properly evacuated before charging"
  ]
}
